content:
# 5-Persona Review: Multi-Spoke Design (2026-03-28)

## Verdicts: 4x "With fixes", 1x "No" (Security)

## 8 Critical Issues
- HubContext panics on unmapped paths
- No duplicate spoke name guard
- OAuth token expiry in long-running spokes
- No rollback on partial creation failure
- HubContext fails outside containers
- Non-deterministic Docker network selection
- MCP endpoint on 0.0.0.0 with NO authentication (Security blocker)
- No failure UX for headless spoke operations

## 13 Important Issues
GITHUB_TOKEN validation, resource limits, agent identity spoofing, supply chain (npm @latest), token scope, test plan, startup error reporting, clone depth, usage agreement, branch naming, hub restart resilience, disk exhaustion, spoke permissions

## Key Security Findings
MCP endpoint has zero auth. Any container on Docker network can write/delete knowledge, change config, run GC. Must add bearer token auth before deployment. Agent identity is self-declared env var with no server-side verification.

## Consensus: Architecture is sound, hardening needed before production.

Doc: docs/decisions/2026-03-28-spoke-design-review-summary.md
